content.problemStatement:
Given an array A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​, count the number of subarrays of array AAA which are non-decreasing.

A subarray A[i,j]A[i, j]A[i,j], where 1≤i≤j≤N1 ≤ i ≤ j ≤ N1≤i≤j≤N is a sequence of integers Ai{A_i}Ai​, Ai+1A_{i+1}Ai+1​, ..., Aj{A_j}Aj​.

A subarray A[i,j]A[i, j]A[i,j] is non-decreasing if Ai≤Ai+1≤Ai+2≤...≤AjA_i ≤ A_{i+1} ≤ A_{i+2} ≤ ... ≤ A_jAi​≤Ai+1​≤Ai+2​≤...≤Aj​. You have to count the total number of such subarrays.
content.inputFormat: The first line of input contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N denoting the size of array. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN denoting the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output in a single line the required answer, which is the total number of non-decreasing subarrays.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the non-decreasing subarrays are [1], [1, 4], [4], [2], [2, 3], [3]. For the second test case, the only non-decreasing subarray is [5].

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use dynamic programming. We maintain a DP array where DP[i] represents the number of non-decreasing subarrays ending at index i. We initialize DP[i] to 1 for all i, because a single element is always a non-decreasing subarray. Then, for each element, we check if it is greater than or equal to the previous element. If it is, we update DP[i] by adding DP[i-1] to it, because all non-decreasing subarrays ending at i-1 can be extended to i. Finally, we sum up all DP[i] values to get the total number of non-decreasing subarrays.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum size of the array. This is because we process each element in the array once for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — because we need to store the DP array of size N for each test case.

code.Python3:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
for _ in range(int(input())):
    N = int(input())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    dp = [1] * N
    ans = 1
    for i in range(1, N):
        if arr[i-1] <= arr[i]:
            dp[i] += dp[i-1]
        ans += dp[i]
    print(ans)
